page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.medium is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a single dedicated AWS Graviton processor core based on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture (Neoverse-V1) running at 2.6 GHz. The CPU includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. It is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks reflect its single-core configuration. Single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place the instance in the average tier, demonstrating stable single-threaded execution. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core, score in the lower tier due to the single-vCPU limitation. Memory bandwidth tests show 96.93 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 31.16 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-threaded compression tasks perform moderately well, with gzip compression reaching 23.7 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7gn.medium offers a low-entry point to the Graviton7 family, providing dedicated ARM resources and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps without the overhead of multi-tenant CPU sharing. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, network-optimized microservices, basic utility scripting, and development or testing environments optimized for the ARM64 architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.medium is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor core running at 2.6 GHz. It features 2.0 GB of system memory, a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Lacking local storage and GPU hardware, it relies on network-attached storage. Benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU performance, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are limited by the single-vCPU configuration. This instance offers a cost-effective entry point for ARM64 development, lightweight single-threaded microservices, and network-optimized utility workloads.

tagline: An entry-level, compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a dedicated processor core and high-speed network optimization for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

2.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for network-heavy tasks
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Best suited for lightweight ARM64 development, testing, and microservices
